statement.markdown: Implement a bubble sort algorithm to sort an array of integers in ascending order. However, the implementation contains a subtle logical bug that prevents it from working correctly. Your task is to identify and fix the bug to make the algorithm work correctly.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of integers, for example: [5, 3, 8, 1, 2]
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a sorted array of integers in ascending order, for example: [1, 2, 3, 5, 8]
statement.notes: The algorithm should be able to handle arrays of varying sizes and should be efficient in terms of time complexity.

constraints.description: The size of the input array

examples.explanation: The input array is sorted in ascending order using the bubble sort algorithm.

hints.text: Check the termination condition of the algorithm.
Verify the comparison logic in the inner loop.

editorial.title: Bubble Sort Algorithm
editorial.intuition: The bubble sort algorithm works by repeatedly iterating through the array and swapping adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to use a nested loop to compare each pair of elements and swap them if necessary.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient approach is to use a flag to track whether any swaps were made in the last iteration.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a flag to track whether any swaps were made in the last iteration and to terminate the algorithm early if no swaps were made.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the algorithm can be proven by showing that it satisfies the properties of a sorting algorithm.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the algorithm can help identify the bug.
commonMistakes: Failing to terminate the algorithm early when no swaps are made.
Incorrect comparison logic in the inner loop.
